evidence_quotes.quote: Protection against interference with privacy, regardless of the pretext.

Ensuring the long-term availability of already purchased digital products (both software and, for example, series suddenly denied to the public for tax reasons, it is in the interest of both the general public and creators that art (broadly) does not disappear).

Protection against payment processors that distort the market through moralist rules and harm both consumers and creators to destroy certain categories of content that do not fit into a fundamentalist, illiberal world image.

paragraphs.text: Dear Commission, My concerns on digital consumer protection are, in no particular order: Protection against misleading AI systems (as the problem of hallucinations and the like is not solved, but probably impossible with this technology, this includes all LLMs). Ensuring the long-term availability of already purchased digital products (both software and, for example, series suddenly denied to the public for tax reasons, it is in the interest of both the general public and creators that art (broadly) does not disappear). Protection against payment processors that distort the market through moralist rules and harm both consumers and creators to destroy certain categories of content that do not fit into a fundamentalist, illiberal world image. Protection against interference with privacy, regardless of the pretext. Thank you very much.
